source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)
notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — recording deferred (no aider auth in env). Aider's bare, argument-less local slash commands (/tokens, /help, /cost, /status) respond from in-process code with NO model call. Unlike claudecode (whose isLocalCommandContent filter drops the <local-command> wrapper so the session stays flat at ready), aider logs the raw slash invocation as a `#### /tokens` user_message (io.user_input writes it before command dispatch), so the parser sets turnOpen=true → ready→working. The command emits only a `>` blockquote via tool_output (skipped by the parser — no `> Tokens:` close, so no assistant_message and no tool_result), then IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done after ~1.5s quiet (clamped to the SessionDetector ~5s working-refresh tick) → back to ready. Net topology is the canonical [ready, working, ready] with ZERO tool calls and no waiting — outcome matches the verify block; the intra-window working flicker is aider-specific (no <local-command> filter). FilesUnderCWD: the daemon keys the whole session by proc-<PID> (transcript .aider.chat.history.md materializes lazily on the first `####` line); the bare slash IS that first line, so it both materializes the transcript AND drives the single working arc. session_birth is anchored to start UNPINNED so the transient proc-<PID> presession ready row can't steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; the slash turn establishes the session identity that later phases chain off.

text: proc-<PID> session appears in ready within 2s of aider launch (process scanner; transcript not yet materialized)
Bare local /tokens slash is logged as a `#### /tokens` user prompt, driving ready → working — even though it makes no model call
Spec: state returns to ready and stays there — the local slash produced no assistant turn (IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done) and no waiting question; no tool call fires
Single process_exited on the proc-<PID> session at REPL teardown (a local slash does not mint a new session)
